Included modules

  1. Setup Personalization
  2. Web Sitemap
  3. Web Schemas
  4. Data Streams
  5. Identity Resolution
  6. Item Data Graphs
  7. Profile Data Graphs

Remaining Partner Workshop labs are covered in the hands-on session.

Developer · Intermediate · ~1 hr 10 mins

Web Sitemap

On This Page
  • Introduction
  • Configure Sitemap Builder
  • Mapping Consent Elements
  • Mapping Page Types
  • Mapping Resolvers
    • Map Resolvers on Product Pages
    • Map Resolvers on Contact Page

    • Create Product View Event
    • Create Add to Cart Event
    • Create Purchase Event
    • Create Contact Form Submissions Event
    • Add Events to Pages
  • Additional Resources

Introduction

In this workshop, you will create a website sitemap using the low-code Sitemap Builder Chrome extension, which allows you to focus on defining what should be tracked on the website without needing to manage the underlying sitemap language and schema creation.

Before starting the hands-on exercises in this workshop, watch the video below to learn important sitemap concepts and components in Data 360.

Enter the password

LearnSPtoday
to play the video.

Configure Sitemap Builder

  1. Click the icon from the top setup menu and select Data Cloud Setup.
  2. In the quick find field, enter
    Websites
    and select Websites & Mobile Apps from the navigation menu.
  3. Click the Training Site connection you created in the Sitemap workshop.
  4. Scroll to the bottom of the page and copy the Script URL.
  5. Open the demo website
  6. Open the Sitemap Builder Chrome extension.
  7. Optionally, click the puzzle icon in your browser toolbar, then click the pin icon next to the Sitemap Builder extension to keep the extension icon visible in your toolbar.
  1. Click the Inject tab.
  2. Paste the Script URL from step 4 in the Web SDK URL field.
  3. Click Inject.
  1. Click the Configure tab.
  2. With the Get Started tab active, select the Start from Scratch tile.
  3. Click Select and Start.

Mapping Consent Elements

To enable the Data 360 SDK to capture behavioral data and allow the Personalization module to return personalized decision responses, you must configure consent management and define CSS selectors that identify specific consent elements on the website.

  1. Click the Consent link in the footer of the demo website.
  2. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Consent Management tab.
  3. Enter
    Website
    in the Consent Provider Name field.
  4. Click Save.
  5. Click the Pick button next to the Opt-In Element field.
  6. Click the green Opt In button in the website dialog to populate the Opt-In Element field with the CSS selector.
  7. Click Save.
  8. Click the Pick button next to the Opt-Out Element field.
  9. Click the red Opt Out button in the website dialog to populate the Opt-Out Element field.
  1. Click Save.

Mapping Page Types

Page types allow you to define groups of similar pages on your website so that personalization experiences can be easily targeted across all pages of the same type. Each page type can also include item data or trigger specific events that you may want to track—these will be configured in later exercises.

In this exercise, you will use Sitemap Builder to create four page types and define the url matching strategy for each page sypte.

  1. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Page Types tab.
  2. Click Add New Page Type.
  3. Populate the configuration fields for the home page based on the following values:
    • Name:
      Home Page
    • Match Strategy: Includes
    • Pathname:
      /
    • On Page Load Interaction: None
  4. Click Save.
  5. Click Add New Page Type.
  6. Populate the configuration fields for product detail pages based on the following values:
    • Name:
      Product Detail Page
    • Match Strategy: Includes
    • Pathname:
      /product/
    • On Page Load Interaction: None
  7. Click Save.
  8. Click Add New Page Type.
  9. Populate the configuration fields for the cart page based on the following values:
    • Name:
      Cart Page
    • Match Strategy: Includes
    • Pathname:
      /cart
    • On Page Load Interaction: None
  10. Click Save.
  11. Click Add New Page Type.
  12. Populate the configuration fields for the contact page based on the following values:
    • Name:
      Contact Us Page
    • Match Strategy: Includes
    • Pathname:
      /contact
    • On Page Load Interaction: None
  13. Click Save.
  14. Navigate to the relevant page URLs on the website and observe how each URL’s pathname matches the pattern you defined for the corresponding page types.

Mapping Resolvers

In Salesforce Personalization, resolvers are JavaScript functions used within the Sitemap to safely extract data from a web page's Document Object Model (DOM), meta tags, or JavaScript objects. They are part of the Web SDK and allow the platform to capture specific attributes—such as product names, prices, user IDs, or page types—to build a comprehensive, real-time profile of the user.

In this exercise, you will use the Sitemap Builder to select page elements that represent the respective page attributes.

Capturing Data

This exercise uses CSS selectors to capture data for simplicity. In a typical implementation, a customer’s data layer might be a more reliable source for resolver configuration. Data points from the data layer are easily accessible via custom JavaScript.

Map Resolvers on Product Pages

  1. Open any product detail page on the demo website.
  2. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Resolvers tab.
  3. Click Add New Resolver.
  4. Enter
    Product Id
    in the Name field.
  5. Leave the default values selected in the Kind, Method, and Output Type fields.
  6. Leave CSS Selector (text) selected in the Method field.
  7. Leave Text selected in the Output Type field.
  8. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  9. Select the product SKU on a product detail page (see below) to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Product Name
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave the default values selected in the Kind, Method, and Output Type fields.
  5. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  6. Select the product name on a product detail page to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Category
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave the default values selected in the Kind, Method, and Output Type fields.
  5. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  6. Select the product SKU on a product detail page to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Product Price
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave Built-in selected in the Kind field.
  5. Leave CSS Selector (text) selected in the Method field.
  6. Select Number from the Output Type field.

Set Output Type

Ensure you change the Output Type to Number to ensure the product price is converted from a string to a number.

  1. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  2. Select the product price on a product detail page to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.

Map Resolvers on Contact Page

  1. Open the Contact page on the demo website.
  2. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Resolvers tab.
  3. Click Add New Resolver.
  4. Enter
    Contact Name
    in the Name field.
  5. Leave the default values selected in the Kind, Method, and Output Type fields.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Select the name field in the contact form to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Contact Email
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave the default values selected in the Kind, Method, and Output Type fields.
  5. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  6. Select the name field in the contact form to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.

Map Resolvers on Cart Page

  1. Add a few products to the cart on the demo website.
  2. Open the cart page.
  3. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Resolvers tab.
  4. Enter
    Order Id
    in the Name field.
  5. Leave the default values selected in the Kind, Method, and Output Type fields.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Select the order id value to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Order Total
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave the default values selected in the Kind and Method fields.
  5. Select Number from the Output Type field.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Select the total value to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Line Item Id
    in the Name field.
  4. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  5. Select any SKU in an order line item row to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Line Item Qty
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave the default values selected in the Kind and Method fields.
  5. Select Number from the Output Type field.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Select a quantity in an order line item row to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.
  2. Click Add New Resolver.
  3. Enter
    Line Item Price
    in the Name field.
  4. Leave the default values selected in the Kind and Method fields.
  5. Select Number from the Output Type field.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Select a line item price in an order line item row to populate the selector field.
  1. Click Save.

Create Events

With your item and profile data now defined, the next step is to map the key events a customer can perform on your website. Events can be simple—such as viewing the homepage—or more detailed—such as adding a product to the cart or completing a purchase, where each event includes item attributes you configured earlier.

Create Product View Event

In this exercise, you will use a standard event to capture product page views.

  1. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Events tab.
  2. Click Add New Event.
  3. Select the Standard Event card.
  4. Populate the configuration fields based on the following values:
    • Label:
      Product View
    • Category: Category
    • Interaction: View Catalog Object
    • Catalog Object Type:
      Product
    • ID Selector: Product Id
  5. Click Add Attribute in the Interaction Attributes section and populate the following values:
    • Key:
      Price
    • Type: Resolver
    • Value: Product Price
  6. Click Add Attribute and populate the following values:
    • Key:
      Name
    • Type: Resolver
    • Value: Product Name
  1. Scroll up and click Save.

Create Add to Cart Event

In this exercise, you will create a standard event to track add to cart events.

  1. Click Add New Event.
  2. Select the Standard Event card.
  3. Populate the configuration fields based on the following values:
    • Label:
      Add to Cart
    • Category: Cart
    • Interaction: Add To Cart
    • Catalog Object Type:
      Product
    • Line Items Mode: Single item
    • Cart ID Selector: [leave unselected]
    • Catalog Object Type: Product
    • Catalog Object ID Resolver: [leave unselected]
    • Quantity Resolver: [leave unselected]
    • Price Resolver: [leave unselected]
  4. Scroll up and click Save.

Create Purchase Event

In this exercise, you will create a standard event to track cart purchase events.

  1. Click Add New Event.
  2. Select the Standard Event card.
  3. Populate the configuration fields based on the following values:
    • Label:
      Purchase
    • Category: Order
    • Interaction: Purchase
    • Order ID Selector: Order Id
    • Total Value Selector: Order Total
    • Line Items Selector:
      .order-item
    • Catalog Object Type: Line Item Id
    • Catalog Object ID Resolver: Line Item Price
    • Quantity Resolver: Line Item Qty
    • Price Resolver: Line Item Price
  4. Scroll up and click Save.

Create Contact Form Submissions Event

In this exercise, you will create a custom event to capture contact form submissions.

  1. Click Add New Event.
  2. Select the Custom Event card.
  3. Populate the configuration fields based on the following values:
    • Label:
      Form Fill
    • Event Type:
      userEngagement
  4. In the Profile Attributes section, select the email capture resolver you defined earlier.
  5. Scroll down to the Preview section and verify that the displayed code matches the example shown below. If there are any differences, update your configuration before proceeding.
{
  interaction: {
    name: "Form Fill",
    eventType: "userEngagement",
    attributes: {
      eventType: "contactPointEmail",
      email: contactemail(),
      name: contactname()
    }
  }
}
  1. Click Save.

Using Sitemap Data Capture to Power Real-Time Identity Resolution

When building a sitemap, it’s important to identify opportunities to capture key identifiers such as an email address. Login experiences, form submissions, email capture pop-ups, and similar interactions are all effective ways to convert an anonymous visitor into a known individual—unlocking more meaningful cross-channel personalization. In a later workshop, you’ll see how the email value captured in this exercise helps fuel real-time identity resolution.

Add Events to Pages

With events configured, you can now add them to the page types where they should fire.

  1. Open any product page on the demo website, for example, Carbon Road Bike.
  2. Open Sitemap Builder and click on the Page Types tab.
  3. Select the Product Detail Page from the Page Types menu.
  4. Select Product View from the On Page Load Interaction menu.
  5. Click Add Listener.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Click the Add to Cart button on the product page to populate the Selector field.
  8. Select Add to Cart from the Event to Send menu.
  1. Scroll up and click Save.
  2. Click the Cart link in the top navigation menu on the demo website.
  3. Open Sitemap Builder and select the Cart Page from the Page Types menu.
  4. Select Cart Page View from the On Page Load Interaction menu.
  5. Click Add Listener.
  6. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  7. Click the Proceed to Checkout button on the cart page to populate the Selector field.
  8. Select Purchase from the Event to Send menu.
  9. Scroll up and click Save.
  10. Click the Contact link in the top navigation menu on the demo website.
  11. Open Sitemap Builder and select the Contact Us Page from the Page Types menu.
  12. Select Form Fill from the On Page Load Interaction menu.
  13. Click Add Listener.
  14. Click the Pick button next to the Selector field.
  15. Click the Send Message button on the contact page to populate the Selector field.
  16. Select Form Fill from the Event to Send menu.
  17. Scroll up and click Save.
